intent: Check whether the candidate can explain gRPC's service contract model, client stubs, server implementations, and why Protocol Buffers are commonly used as the IDL and message format.
primaryQuestion: How would you explain the basic gRPC flow from a .proto service definition to a client calling a server method?

expectedSignals.label: Explains that a .proto file defines services, RPC methods, request messages, and response messages.

requirements.description: Explains that the .proto file defines service names and RPC methods
Explains that each RPC method refers to typed request and response messages
Treats the .proto file as the shared API contract between client and server

expectedSignals.label: Mentions generated client stubs and server interfaces or base classes.

requirements.description: Mentions generated client stub code used by callers
Mentions generated server interfaces, base classes, or registration code implemented by the service
Mentions code generation for the chosen language rather than hand-writing wire parsing

expectedSignals.label: Understands that the client calls a remote method through the stub while the server implements the service contract.

requirements.description: Explains that the client invokes a method on the generated stub
Explains that the server implements and registers the generated service contract
Distinguishes local stub method invocation from the remote network call handled by gRPC

expectedSignals.label: Connects Protocol Buffers to strongly typed, language-neutral message serialization rather than treating gRPC as plain JSON over HTTP.

requirements.description: Mentions Protocol Buffers as the normal binary serialization format for gRPC messages
Connects protobuf fields and generated classes to strongly typed request and response data
Explains that different implementation languages can share the same .proto contract

scoringHints.description: Clearly walks through .proto definition, generated code, client stub call, server handler, serialized request/response, and status completion.
Explains service definitions and stubs but misses one runtime detail such as status or metadata.
Knows gRPC is for service communication but cannot explain the contract-to-code flow.
Answer is mostly buzzwords and does not show practical gRPC basics.

followUps.question: What files or classes would be generated from a simple Greeter .proto?
followUps.purpose: Force concrete understanding of code generation and usage.

followUps.question: What changes on the client if the server is written in a different programming language?

en.title: gRPC contract and stub basics
en.displayQuestion: How would you explain the basic gRPC flow from a .proto service definition to a client calling a server method?
en.displayIntent: Check gRPC service contract, generated stubs, and Protocol Buffers fundamentals.
guidance: Ask for the flow from .proto to generated code to runtime call.
Listen for a clear distinction between contract, generated client, and server implementation.
commonMistakes: Describing gRPC as plain REST or only as serialization.
Skipping generated stubs and server service implementation.

vi.title: Nền tảng contract và stub trong gRPC
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn giải thích luồng gRPC cơ bản từ một service definition trong file .proto đến lúc client gọi method trên server như thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra nền tảng service contract, generated stub và Protocol Buffers trong gRPC.
guidance: Yêu cầu ứng viên đi từ .proto tới generated code rồi tới runtime call.
Nghe xem ứng viên có tách rõ contract, generated client và server implementation hay không.
commonMistakes: Mô tả gRPC như REST thông thường hoặc chỉ là cơ chế serialize.
Bỏ qua generated stub và phần implement service phía server.

ja.title: gRPC contract と stub の基礎
ja.displayQuestion: .proto の service definition から client が server method を呼ぶまでの基本的な gRPC flow をどう説明しますか。
ja.displayIntent: gRPC service contract、generated stubs、Protocol Buffers の基礎を確認する。
guidance: .proto から generated code、runtime call までの流れを説明させる。
contract、generated client、server implementation の区別に注目する。
commonMistakes: gRPC を通常の REST や serialization だけとして説明する。
generated stub と server service implementation を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Introduction to gRPC
sourceReferences.note: Used for the service definition, client stub, server implementation, and Protocol Buffers overview.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Core concepts, architecture and lifecycle
sourceReferences.note: Used for service definition and RPC lifecycle concepts.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate understands unary, server streaming, client streaming, and bidirectional streaming RPCs and can choose the right shape for a use case.
primaryQuestion: What are the four gRPC RPC types, and when would you choose each one?

expectedSignals.label: Identifies unary, server streaming, client streaming, and bidirectional streaming RPCs.

requirements.description: Names unary RPC as one request followed by one response
Names both server streaming and client streaming RPC types
Names bidirectional streaming as both sides sending streams of messages

expectedSignals.label: Maps unary to simple request/response interactions.

requirements.description: Maps unary RPC to simple operations such as lookup, create, update, or command-style request response
States that the server returns a single response for a unary call
Avoids choosing streaming when a single request and response is enough

expectedSignals.label: Maps server streaming to one request producing many ordered responses.

requirements.description: Defines server streaming as one client request followed by multiple server messages
Applies server streaming to a case such as live dashboard updates or incremental result delivery
Mentions ordered messages within the stream from the server to the client

expectedSignals.label: Maps client streaming or bidirectional streaming to uploads, live feeds, chat, telemetry, or interactive sessions.

requirements.description: Maps client streaming to many client messages followed by a final server response, such as upload or telemetry aggregation
Maps bidirectional streaming to interactive flows such as chat, live collaboration, or duplex telemetry
Explains when the server normally sends a response in client streaming after the client finishes or half-closes the stream

scoringHints.description: Accurately explains all four RPC shapes and gives realistic examples with completion and ordering behavior.
Names all four types and gives mostly correct examples but misses lifecycle nuance.
Understands request/response but confuses client, server, and bidirectional streaming.
Answer does not show reliable knowledge of gRPC RPC types.

followUps.question: For a dashboard that receives live order updates, which RPC type would you use and why?
followUps.purpose: Check whether the candidate can map RPC type to product behavior.

followUps.question: In client streaming, when does the server normally send its response?

en.title: gRPC RPC type selection
en.displayQuestion: What are the four gRPC RPC types, and when would you choose each one?
en.displayIntent: Check practical understanding of unary and streaming RPC shapes.
guidance: Ask for concrete use cases, not only definitions.
Listen for who sends one message versus many messages.
commonMistakes: Treating all streaming modes as identical.
Using bidirectional streaming when unary or server streaming is enough.

vi.title: Chọn loại RPC trong gRPC
vi.displayQuestion: Bốn loại RPC trong gRPC là gì, và khi nào bạn chọn từng loại?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra hiểu biết thực tế về unary RPC và các dạng streaming RPC.
guidance: Yêu cầu ví dụ use case cụ thể, không chỉ định nghĩa.
Nghe xem ứng viên phân biệt được bên nào gửi một message và bên nào gửi nhiều message.
commonMistakes: Xem mọi dạng streaming là giống nhau.
Dùng bidirectional streaming trong khi unary hoặc server streaming đã đủ.

ja.title: gRPC RPC type の選択
ja.displayQuestion: gRPC の 4 つの RPC type は何で、それぞれいつ選びますか。
ja.displayIntent: unary と streaming RPC の実践的な理解を確認する。
guidance: 定義だけでなく具体的な use case を聞く。
どちら側が 1 message / 複数 message を送るかに注目する。
commonMistakes: すべての streaming mode を同じものとして扱う。
unary や server streaming で十分な場面に bidirectional streaming を使う。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Core concepts, architecture and lifecycle
sourceReferences.note: Used for unary, server streaming, client streaming, bidirectional streaming, ordering, and lifecycle behavior.

intent: Assess basic troubleshooting of gRPC failures using status codes, deadlines, logs, and client/server evidence.
primaryQuestion: A gRPC client starts receiving DEADLINE_EXCEEDED and UNAVAILABLE errors after deployment. What would you check first?

expectedSignals.label: Reads the exact status code, message, method name, deadline, and timing before changing code.

requirements.description: Captures the exact gRPC status code and error message instead of grouping all failures together
Identifies the affected method name, configured deadline, and elapsed time
Compares the error start time with deployment, config, traffic, or infrastructure changes

expectedSignals.label: Separates DEADLINE_EXCEEDED from UNAVAILABLE and explains likely causes for each.

requirements.description: Explains DEADLINE_EXCEEDED as the client deadline expiring before a response is received
Explains UNAVAILABLE as a transport, endpoint, load balancer, server availability, or transient connectivity problem
Warns that retrying non-idempotent calls after UNAVAILABLE can duplicate side effects if the server may have processed the request

expectedSignals.label: Checks client deadline configuration, server latency, network path, DNS, load balancer, and deployment health.

requirements.description: Checks whether client deadline settings became too short for real server latency
Checks server latency, saturation, deployment health, and application errors
Checks DNS, name resolution, load balancer behavior, routing, and network path changes

expectedSignals.label: Uses logs, metrics, traces, or request IDs to compare client-side and server-side evidence.

requirements.description: Uses request IDs, trace IDs, or correlation metadata to compare client and server logs
Uses per-method latency and status-code metrics to identify whether failures are broad or method-specific
Looks for evidence that the server completed work after the client deadline expired

scoringHints.description: Uses status-specific diagnosis across deadline configuration, server latency, network availability, and deployment health with evidence.
Checks basic logs and timeout values but misses one layer such as load balancer or service health.
Mentions generic debugging but lacks gRPC status-code reasoning.
No clear investigation sequence or gRPC-specific signals.

followUps.question: What evidence would tell you the server completed work but the client deadline expired?
When is retrying an UNAVAILABLE response unsafe?

en.title: Debug gRPC status failures
en.displayQuestion: A gRPC client starts receiving DEADLINE_EXCEEDED and UNAVAILABLE errors after deployment. What would you check first?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate status-code-based debugging for gRPC failures.
guidance: Expect the candidate to start from concrete error evidence.
Ask them to separate timeout, server latency, and availability causes.
commonMistakes: Increasing deadlines without root cause evidence.
Blindly retrying all failed calls.

vi.title: Debug lỗi status trong gRPC
vi.displayQuestion: Một gRPC client bắt đầu nhận lỗi DEADLINE_EXCEEDED và UNAVAILABLE sau khi deploy. Bạn sẽ kiểm tra gì trước?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug lỗi gRPC dựa trên status code.
guidance: Kỳ vọng ứng viên bắt đầu từ bằng chứng lỗi cụ thể.
Yêu cầu tách nguyên nhân timeout, server latency và availability.
commonMistakes: Tăng deadline mà không có bằng chứng root cause.
Retry mọi failed call một cách mù quáng.

ja.title: gRPC status failure の debug
ja.displayQuestion: deploy 後に gRPC client が DEADLINE_EXCEEDED と UNAVAILABLE を受け始めました。最初に何を確認しますか。
ja.displayIntent: gRPC failure を status code ベースで debug できるか評価する。
guidance: 具体的な error evidence から始めることを期待する。
timeout、server latency、availability の原因を分けて説明させる。
commonMistakes: root cause の証拠なしに deadline だけを伸ばす。
すべての failed call を盲目的に retry する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Status Codes
sourceReferences.note: Used for status code meanings including DEADLINE_EXCEEDED and UNAVAILABLE.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Deadlines
sourceReferences.note: Used for deadline behavior, cancellation, and client/server timeout reasoning.

intent: Evaluate whether the candidate can evolve Protocol Buffer contracts safely across independently deployed clients and servers.
primaryQuestion: How would you change a gRPC .proto message safely when old clients and servers may still be running?

expectedSignals.label: Uses additive, backward-compatible changes where possible.

requirements.description: Prefers adding new fields instead of changing or removing existing fields
Ensures old clients can ignore unknown fields and continue working
Ensures old servers can receive messages from newer clients without breaking required behavior

expectedSignals.label: Does not reuse or renumber field tags and reserves deleted field numbers and names.

requirements.description: States that deleted field numbers must not be reused because old serialized data or clients may interpret them incorrectly
Avoids renumbering existing field tags even if field names change
Uses reserved field numbers and names after deleting fields

expectedSignals.label: Avoids changing field types or changing repeated fields to scalar fields.

requirements.description: Avoids changing an existing field's wire-incompatible type
Avoids changing repeated fields to scalar fields or the reverse without a migration strategy
Uses a new field number when a field needs a different shape or semantics

expectedSignals.label: Plans rollout across generated code, server compatibility, client compatibility, and validation before removing old fields.

requirements.description: Plans regeneration and release of server and client code in a compatible order
Keeps old and new clients and servers compatible during the rollout window
Validates usage has moved away from old fields before removing or reserving them

scoringHints.description: Explains wire compatibility, reserved tags/names, additive rollout, generated code updates, and deprecation/removal sequencing.
Understands not to reuse field numbers but misses rollout or JSON/name compatibility details.
Knows .proto files can change but lacks compatibility rules.
No safe schema evolution plan is demonstrated.

followUps.question: Why is reusing a deleted protobuf field number dangerous?
What rollout sequence would you use if a response field must eventually be replaced?

en.title: Safe protobuf schema evolution
en.displayQuestion: How would you change a gRPC .proto message safely when old clients and servers may still be running?
en.displayIntent: Check compatibility discipline for evolving protobuf contracts.
guidance: Ask for concrete compatible and incompatible changes.
Expect a deployment sequence, not only schema rules.
commonMistakes: Reusing field numbers or changing existing field meanings.
Assuming every client upgrades at the same time.

vi.title: Evolve protobuf schema an toàn
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn thay đổi một message .proto của gRPC an toàn như thế nào khi client và server phiên bản cũ vẫn có thể đang chạy?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra kỷ luật compatibility khi evolve protobuf contract.
guidance: Yêu cầu ví dụ cụ thể về thay đổi compatible và incompatible.
Kỳ vọng có trình tự deployment, không chỉ nêu rule schema.
commonMistakes: Reuse field number hoặc đổi ý nghĩa field cũ.
Giả định mọi client đều upgrade cùng lúc.

ja.title: 安全な protobuf schema evolution
ja.displayQuestion: 古い client と server がまだ動いている可能性があるとき、gRPC の .proto message をどう安全に変更しますか。
ja.displayIntent: protobuf contract を evolve する際の compatibility discipline を確認する。
guidance: compatible / incompatible change の具体例を聞く。
schema rule だけでなく deployment sequence を期待する。
commonMistakes: field number を再利用したり既存 field の意味を変えたりする。
すべての client が同時に upgrade すると仮定する。

sourceReferences.label: Protocol Buffers Docs: Proto Best Practices
sourceReferences.note: Used for field number reuse, reserved tags, type changes, required fields, and compatibility practices.

sourceReferences.label: Protocol Buffers Docs: Language Guide proto3
sourceReferences.note: Used for field number rules and reserved field guidance.

intent: Assess practical use of gRPC metadata for authentication, tracing, correlation, and response trailers without abusing it as a business payload channel.
primaryQuestion: How would you use gRPC metadata for authentication and request correlation, and what should not go into metadata?

expectedSignals.label: Explains metadata as RPC-associated key-value data often transmitted as HTTP/2 headers.

requirements.description: Explains metadata as key-value data attached to an RPC rather than part of the protobuf message body
Mentions that metadata is commonly transported as HTTP/2 headers or trailers
Distinguishes request or initial metadata from response trailing metadata when relevant

expectedSignals.label: Uses metadata for auth tokens, tenant or correlation IDs, trace context, or server trailers when appropriate.

requirements.description: Uses metadata for authentication credentials such as bearer tokens when protected by TLS and server-side validation
Uses metadata for trace context, correlation IDs, request IDs, or tenant routing context when appropriate
Mentions server trailers for status details or response-side metadata when useful

expectedSignals.label: Avoids putting large business payloads, sensitive data without protection, or fields that belong in the protobuf request into metadata.

requirements.description: Keeps business fields that affect domain behavior in typed protobuf request messages
Avoids putting large payloads or frequently changing business data into metadata
Avoids logging or transmitting sensitive metadata without protection and redaction

expectedSignals.label: Mentions key constraints such as reserved grpc- prefix, ASCII keys, binary keys ending in -bin, and size/header limits.

requirements.description: Mentions that grpc- prefixed metadata keys are reserved
Mentions ASCII metadata keys and binary metadata keys ending in -bin
Mentions header or metadata size limits as a reason not to misuse metadata for payloads

scoringHints.description: Balances auth, tracing, and trailers with typed API design, security, key conventions, and operational limits.
Understands auth metadata but misses binary/size constraints or trailer usage.
Knows metadata exists but treats it like arbitrary request body data.
No practical understanding of metadata usage is shown.

followUps.question: Where would you carry a user ID used by business logic: metadata or the request message, and why?
How would you debug missing auth metadata without leaking secrets?

en.title: gRPC metadata for auth and tracing
en.displayQuestion: How would you use gRPC metadata for authentication and request correlation, and what should not go into metadata?
en.displayIntent: Check practical metadata usage and boundaries.
guidance: Expect examples such as auth, trace context, correlation IDs, and trailers.
Probe for security and typed request-body boundaries.
commonMistakes: Using metadata as an untyped business payload channel.
Logging tokens or ignoring reserved key conventions.

vi.title: Metadata gRPC cho auth và tracing
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn dùng gRPC metadata cho authentication và request correlation như thế nào, và những gì không nên đưa vào metadata?
vi.displayIntent: Kiểm tra cách dùng metadata thực tế và boundary của metadata.
guidance: Kỳ vọng ví dụ như auth, trace context, correlation ID và trailer.
Đào sâu security và ranh giới giữa metadata với typed request body.
commonMistakes: Dùng metadata như kênh payload nghiệp vụ không type.
Log token hoặc bỏ qua convention key reserved.

ja.title: auth と tracing のための gRPC metadata
ja.displayQuestion: authentication と request correlation に gRPC metadata をどう使い、metadata に入れるべきでないものは何ですか。
ja.displayIntent: metadata の実践的な使い方と boundary を確認する。
guidance: auth、trace context、correlation ID、trailer などの例を期待する。
security と typed request body との境界を掘り下げる。
commonMistakes: metadata を untyped な business payload channel として使う。
token を log したり reserved key convention を無視したりする。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Metadata
sourceReferences.note: Used for metadata purpose, HTTP/2 header mapping, key conventions, and binary metadata.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for trailing metadata and richer error model context.

intent: Evaluate debugging skill for streaming RPCs involving flow control, slow consumers, cancellation, memory growth, and read/write coordination.
primaryQuestion: A bidirectional streaming gRPC endpoint works in tests but stalls or grows memory under production load. How would you diagnose it?

expectedSignals.label: Checks whether either side is writing faster than the other side reads.

requirements.description: Compares producer write rate with consumer read rate on both client and server sides
Identifies whether a slow client or server reader is causing messages to queue
Determines whether the stall happens on client-to-server, server-to-client, or both stream directions

expectedSignals.label: Understands that gRPC flow control can block writes and that streaming is sensitive to slow consumers.

requirements.description: Explains that gRPC or HTTP/2 flow control can block writes when the peer is not reading fast enough
Distinguishes application write completion from the message actually being consumed by the peer
Connects slow consumers or buffering to memory growth under production load

expectedSignals.label: Looks for missing cancellation handling, unbounded buffering, oversized messages, or failure to drain streams.

requirements.description: Checks that server and client clean up goroutines, listeners, buffers, or subscriptions when a stream is cancelled
Checks for unbounded queues, oversized messages, or missing maximum message size limits
Checks whether streams are drained, half-closed, or closed correctly on errors and shutdown

expectedSignals.label: Uses per-stream metrics, message counts/sizes, deadlines, backpressure logs, and load-test reproduction.

requirements.description: Uses per-stream metrics such as active streams, message counts, message sizes, queue depth, and duration
Uses deadlines, cancellation logs, backpressure logs, and write wait times to locate the bottleneck
Reproduces the stall or memory growth with production-like stream concurrency and message rates

scoringHints.description: Systematically diagnoses flow control, slow readers, cancellation, buffering, message size, and reproduction under load.
Covers slow consumers and logs but misses one important layer such as cancellation or memory buffering.
Mentions generic performance debugging but lacks streaming-specific evidence.
No credible diagnosis path for streaming stalls.

followUps.question: How would you prove whether the server is blocked on write because the client is not reading fast enough?
What cleanup should happen when the client cancels a long-lived stream?

en.title: Debug gRPC streaming flow control
en.displayQuestion: A bidirectional streaming gRPC endpoint works in tests but stalls or grows memory under production load. How would you diagnose it?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate streaming RPC debugging with flow control and cancellation.
guidance: Ask for evidence around slow consumers, blocked writes, and buffering.
Expect cancellation and resource cleanup to be included.
commonMistakes: Treating stream writes as guaranteed network sends.
Ignoring backpressure and unbounded queues.

vi.title: Debug flow control trong gRPC streaming
vi.displayQuestion: Một endpoint gRPC bidirectional streaming chạy ổn trong test nhưng bị stall hoặc tăng memory dưới production load. Bạn sẽ chẩn đoán thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug streaming RPC với flow control và cancellation.
guidance: Hỏi bằng chứng về slow consumer, blocked write và buffering.
Kỳ vọng có xử lý cancellation và cleanup resource.
commonMistakes: Xem stream write như việc message chắc chắn đã được gửi qua network.
Bỏ qua backpressure và queue không giới hạn.

ja.title: gRPC streaming flow control の debug
ja.displayQuestion: bidirectional streaming gRPC endpoint が test では動くのに production load で stall したり memory が増えたりします。どう診断しますか。
ja.displayIntent: flow control と cancellation を含む streaming RPC debug 力を評価する。
guidance: slow consumer、blocked write、buffering の証拠を聞く。
cancellation と resource cleanup を含めることを期待する。
commonMistakes: stream write を network send 完了と同一視する。
backpressure と unbounded queue を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Flow Control
sourceReferences.note: Used for flow control, blocked writes, receiver capacity, and streaming-specific deadlock risks.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Core concepts, architecture and lifecycle
sourceReferences.note: Used for streaming lifecycle and cancellation behavior.

intent: Assess whether the candidate can choose between gRPC and REST based on product, platform, compatibility, operations, and client constraints.
primaryQuestion: When would you choose gRPC over REST for a service API, and when would REST still be the better choice?

expectedSignals.label: Chooses gRPC for strongly typed internal service contracts, efficient binary messages, streaming, and polyglot generated clients.

requirements.description: Chooses gRPC when strong internal service contracts and generated clients are valuable
Mentions efficient protobuf serialization or lower overhead for service-to-service traffic where it matters
Mentions streaming support or polyglot generated clients as concrete reasons to choose gRPC

expectedSignals.label: Chooses REST/HTTP JSON for public browser-facing APIs, broad tooling compatibility, caching semantics, or simple resource-oriented APIs when appropriate.

requirements.description: Chooses REST or HTTP JSON for browser-facing or third-party public APIs when compatibility is more important
Mentions HTTP tooling, caches, gateways, debugging tools, or resource semantics as REST advantages
Matches the protocol choice to API shape instead of defaulting to one technology

expectedSignals.label: Considers operational constraints such as proxies, load balancers, observability, gateway needs, and team familiarity.

requirements.description: Checks whether proxies, ingress, load balancers, and service mesh support HTTP/2 and gRPC behavior
Checks logging, metrics, tracing, status code mapping, and debugging support for gRPC
Considers team familiarity, generated client publishing, gateway needs, and client language support

expectedSignals.label: Does not frame gRPC as universally faster or REST as obsolete.

requirements.description: Avoids claiming gRPC is always faster without considering payload, transport, infrastructure, and client constraints
Recognizes that REST remains a good fit for many public, cacheable, or simple resource APIs
Frames the decision as contextual and evidence-based rather than a technology slogan

scoringHints.description: Makes a context-sensitive choice covering contracts, streaming, performance, client ecosystem, operations, and migration cost.
Identifies core benefits and trade-offs but misses one practical adoption constraint.
Knows high-level differences but makes a technology-preference answer.
No clear technical trade-off reasoning.

followUps.question: How would your answer change for a public API consumed by third-party frontend teams?
followUps.purpose: Check client ecosystem and compatibility reasoning.

followUps.question: What infrastructure would you verify before moving an existing internal REST API to gRPC?

en.title: gRPC versus REST trade-off
en.displayQuestion: When would you choose gRPC over REST for a service API, and when would REST still be the better choice?
guidance: Ask for context-specific constraints, not a winner-takes-all answer.
Expect both product/client and infrastructure considerations.
commonMistakes: Choosing gRPC only because it is newer or binary.
Ignoring public API and browser compatibility needs.

vi.title: Trade-off gRPC và REST
vi.displayQuestion: Khi nào bạn chọn gRPC thay vì REST cho service API, và khi nào REST vẫn là lựa chọn tốt hơn?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng chọn công nghệ API theo bối cảnh thực tế.
guidance: Hỏi constraint theo context, không chấp nhận câu trả lời kiểu công nghệ nào luôn thắng.
Kỳ vọng cân nhắc cả product/client và hạ tầng vận hành.
commonMistakes: Chọn gRPC chỉ vì mới hơn hoặc dùng binary.
Bỏ qua nhu cầu public API và browser compatibility.

ja.title: gRPC と REST の trade-off
ja.displayQuestion: service API に gRPC を REST より選ぶのはどんな場合で、REST の方がよいのはどんな場合ですか。
ja.displayIntent: 実践的な API technology selection を評価する。
guidance: 常にどちらかが勝つ回答ではなく context-specific constraint を聞く。
product/client と infrastructure の両方の考慮を期待する。
commonMistakes: 新しい、または binary だからという理由だけで gRPC を選ぶ。
public API や browser compatibility を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Introduction to gRPC
sourceReferences.note: Used for gRPC contract, multi-language, and Protocol Buffers capabilities.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Blog: gRPC Load Balancing
sourceReferences.note: Used for HTTP/2, multiplexing, binary protocol, and deployment considerations.

intent: Evaluate senior-level reliability design around gRPC retries, deadlines, idempotency, retryable status codes, service config, and server pushback.
primaryQuestion: How would you design retry behavior for gRPC calls without causing duplicate side effects or overload?

expectedSignals.label: Separates retry-safe idempotent operations from non-idempotent operations that need idempotency keys or different handling.

requirements.description: Classifies read or idempotent methods as safer candidates for automatic retries
Identifies side-effecting methods such as create order, charge payment, or reserve inventory as unsafe to retry blindly
Uses idempotency keys, request IDs, or deduplication for operations that may be retried after uncertain outcomes

expectedSignals.label: Uses realistic deadlines, retry budgets, exponential backoff, jitter, retryable status codes, and max attempts.

requirements.description: Sets realistic per-call deadlines so retries do not exceed user or upstream time budgets
Uses exponential backoff, jitter, and maximum attempts instead of immediate tight-loop retries
Limits retries to appropriate transient status codes and method policies

expectedSignals.label: Understands that configured retries are per method and that transparent retries may occur only under limited safe conditions.

requirements.description: Mentions that retry behavior should be configured per method or service policy
Mentions that transparent retries are limited to safe cases where the call has not committed at the server application level
Aligns retry policy with each method's side-effect and idempotency semantics

expectedSignals.label: Considers server pushback, retry throttling, observability, and load amplification during incidents.

requirements.description: Considers server pushback, retry throttling, or circuit breaking to avoid worsening outages
Monitors retry attempts, retry success rate, exhausted retries, and status-code distribution
Accounts for retries amplifying load on an already unhealthy backend

scoringHints.description: Designs method-specific retry policy with idempotency boundaries, deadline budgets, backoff/jitter, retry throttling, and metrics.
Understands idempotency and retryable transient failures but misses server pushback or observability.
Uses retries as a generic reliability fix without side-effect discipline.
No safe retry strategy is demonstrated.

followUps.question: Which methods in an order service would you not retry automatically, and what would you use instead?
What retry metrics would you alert on during a backend outage?
How would you decide retry policy per method rather than using one global retry rule?
followUps.purpose: Check method semantics and service config reasoning.

en.title: Safe gRPC retry design
en.displayQuestion: How would you design retry behavior for gRPC calls without causing duplicate side effects or overload?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate senior reliability design for retries and idempotency.
guidance: Ask for method-specific retry policy rather than global retry everywhere.
Expect idempotency, deadlines, backoff, throttling, and metrics.
commonMistakes: Retrying non-idempotent writes blindly.
Creating retry storms with no budget or throttling.

vi.title: Thiết kế retry gRPC an toàn
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn thiết kế retry behavior cho gRPC call như thế nào để không gây duplicate side effect hoặc overload?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá thiết kế reliability cấp senior cho retry và idempotency.
guidance: Yêu cầu retry policy theo từng method, không retry global mọi nơi.
Kỳ vọng có idempotency, deadline, backoff, throttling và metrics.
commonMistakes: Retry non-idempotent write một cách mù quáng.
Tạo retry storm vì không có budget hoặc throttling.

ja.title: 安全な gRPC retry design
ja.displayQuestion: duplicate side effect や overload を起こさずに gRPC call の retry behavior をどう設計しますか。
ja.displayIntent: retry と idempotency に関する senior reliability design を評価する。
guidance: 全体一律ではなく method-specific retry policy を聞く。
idempotency、deadline、backoff、throttling、metrics を期待する。
commonMistakes: non-idempotent write を盲目的に retry する。
budget や throttling なしで retry storm を作る。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Retry
sourceReferences.note: Used for retry policy, transparent retry, backoff, retryable status codes, throttling, and retry observability.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Status Codes
sourceReferences.note: Used for retry-sensitive status code semantics such as UNAVAILABLE and DEADLINE_EXCEEDED.

intent: Assess senior production debugging for gRPC load balancing, health checking, name resolution, connection reuse, and uneven traffic distribution.
primaryQuestion: Traffic to a gRPC service is uneven: one backend is overloaded while others are healthy. How would you diagnose and fix it?

expectedSignals.label: Checks load balancing policy, name resolution results, endpoint discovery, channel reuse, and connection behavior.

requirements.description: Checks the client or proxy load balancing policy in use
Checks resolver or service discovery results to confirm which backends the client can see
Checks channel reuse, subchannel state, connection pooling, and whether all calls are pinned to one connection

expectedSignals.label: Understands that some policies such as pick_first may keep using one ready subchannel instead of spreading calls.

requirements.description: Explains that pick_first can keep using one ready subchannel instead of distributing calls
Compares alternatives such as round_robin, xDS, or proxy-side load balancing when spreading traffic is required
Connects the selected policy and connection behavior to the observed backend imbalance

expectedSignals.label: Verifies server health service behavior, readiness, graceful shutdown, and whether unhealthy backends are removed from traffic.

requirements.description: Checks gRPC health service responses or readiness signals for each backend
Verifies unhealthy or draining backends are removed from endpoint discovery or load balancer rotation
Checks graceful shutdown behavior so existing streams finish and new traffic stops during deploys

expectedSignals.label: Uses per-backend metrics, client channel metrics, resolver logs, load balancer configuration, and controlled rollout verification.

requirements.description: Uses per-backend request rate, latency, error, CPU, and active stream metrics to prove imbalance
Uses client channel metrics, resolver logs, or load balancer config to prove what endpoints were selected
Verifies the fix with a controlled rollout and balanced per-backend traffic after deployment

scoringHints.description: Connects uneven traffic to resolver, LB policy, HTTP/2 connection behavior, health checking, readiness, and metrics-backed remediation.
Good operational debugging flow but misses one senior concern such as graceful shutdown or channel policy.
Focuses only on server capacity and misses gRPC client/load-balancing behavior.
No credible production diagnosis path.

followUps.question: What would you inspect on the client channel or resolver to know which backends it can see?
followUps.purpose: Probe name resolution and client-side LB evidence.

followUps.question: What per-backend metric would prove traffic is balanced after the fix?

en.title: Debug gRPC load balancing
en.displayQuestion: Traffic to a gRPC service is uneven: one backend is overloaded while others are healthy. How would you diagnose and fix it?
en.displayIntent: Evaluate production debugging of gRPC load balancing and health checking.
guidance: Expect client-side and infrastructure-side evidence.
Ask how the candidate would verify the remediation.
commonMistakes: Scaling replicas without checking channel or LB policy.
Ignoring long-lived HTTP/2 connection behavior.

vi.title: Debug load balancing gRPC
vi.displayQuestion: Traffic tới một gRPC service bị lệch: một backend quá tải trong khi các backend khác vẫn khỏe. Bạn sẽ chẩn đoán và sửa thế nào?
vi.displayIntent: Đánh giá khả năng debug production về gRPC load balancing và health checking.
guidance: Kỳ vọng bằng chứng từ cả client-side và infrastructure-side.
Hỏi cách ứng viên verify remediation.
commonMistakes: Scale replica mà không kiểm tra channel hoặc LB policy.
Bỏ qua hành vi long-lived HTTP/2 connection.

ja.title: gRPC load balancing の debug
ja.displayQuestion: gRPC service への traffic が偏り、1 つの backend だけ overload で他は healthy です。どう診断して修正しますか。
ja.displayIntent: gRPC load balancing と health checking の production debug を評価する。
guidance: client-side と infrastructure-side の両方の evidence を期待する。
remediation をどう verify するか聞く。
commonMistakes: channel や LB policy を見ずに replica だけ増やす。
long-lived HTTP/2 connection behavior を無視する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Custom Load Balancing Policies
sourceReferences.note: Used for name resolver, subchannel, policy responsibilities, and pick_first behavior.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Health Checking
sourceReferences.note: Used for server health service, client health checking, and SERVING/NOT_SERVING behavior.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Blog: gRPC Load Balancing
sourceReferences.note: Used for gRPC over HTTP/2 deployment and load balancing context.

intent: Verify senior judgment for owning a production gRPC API, including contract governance, deadlines, errors, observability, deployment safety, and operational readiness.
primaryQuestion: You are taking ownership of a business-critical gRPC API. What production standards would you require before calling it ready?

expectedSignals.label: Defines contract standards: stable .proto ownership, compatibility rules, generated client publishing, and version/deprecation policy.

requirements.description: Defines ownership and review rules for the .proto contract
Defines compatibility rules for adding, deprecating, reserving, and removing fields or methods
Defines generated client publishing, versioning, and deprecation policy for consumers

expectedSignals.label: Requires deadline defaults, cancellation handling, clear status code mapping, and consistent error details where supported.

requirements.description: Requires sensible default deadlines and documented per-method timeout expectations
Requires server-side cancellation handling and cleanup when clients disconnect or deadlines expire
Defines consistent status code mapping and rich error details when the ecosystem supports them

expectedSignals.label: Requires observability: per-method latency, status codes, message sizes, retry attempts, stream counts, traces, logs, and correlation metadata.

requirements.description: Tracks per-method latency, status codes, and error rates
Tracks message sizes, retry attempts, active streams, and stream durations
Adds tracing, correlation metadata, and structured logs for cross-service debugging

expectedSignals.label: Includes operational readiness: health checks, load balancing behavior, graceful shutdown, rollout/rollback plan, capacity tests, and SLO or alert thresholds.

requirements.description: Requires health checks, known load balancing behavior, readiness, and graceful shutdown
Requires rollout and rollback plan plus capacity or load testing for expected traffic
Defines SLOs and alert thresholds tied to per-method latency, errors, saturation, and availability

scoringHints.description: Provides a complete production readiness checklist covering contract governance, reliability behavior, observability, rollout safety, and measurable SLOs.
Covers most readiness areas but misses one senior dimension such as contract publishing or rollback.
Gives a partial checklist focused on implementation details only.
Cannot define production ownership standards for gRPC.

followUps.question: Which three per-method metrics would page you, and what would each imply?
How would you decide whether to use rich error details versus simple status codes for this API?
followUps.purpose: Probe error model and client compatibility trade-offs.

en.title: Production-ready gRPC API ownership
en.displayQuestion: You are taking ownership of a business-critical gRPC API. What production standards would you require before calling it ready?
en.displayIntent: Verify senior ownership of gRPC production readiness.
guidance: Expect contract, reliability, observability, and rollout standards.
Ask for measurable readiness criteria, not only coding practices.
commonMistakes: Only discussing handler implementation.
Skipping deadlines, status codes, metrics, and rollout safety.

vi.title: Ownership API gRPC production-ready
vi.displayQuestion: Bạn nhận ownership một gRPC API critical cho business. Trước khi xem API này production-ready, bạn yêu cầu những standard nào?
vi.displayIntent: Xác minh ownership cấp senior về production readiness cho gRPC.
guidance: Kỳ vọng standard về contract, reliability, observability và rollout.
Hỏi tiêu chí readiness đo được, không chỉ coding practice.
commonMistakes: Chỉ nói về implement handler.
Bỏ qua deadline, status code, metrics và rollout safety.

ja.title: production-ready gRPC API ownership
ja.displayQuestion: business-critical な gRPC API の ownership を持つことになりました。production-ready と呼ぶ前にどんな standard を要求しますか。
ja.displayIntent: gRPC production readiness に対する senior ownership を確認する。
guidance: contract、reliability、observability、rollout standard を期待する。
coding practice だけでなく measurable readiness criteria を聞く。
commonMistakes: handler implementation だけを話す。
deadline、status code、metrics、rollout safety を省略する。

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Deadlines
sourceReferences.note: Used for deadline defaults, propagation, and cancellation behavior.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Error handling
sourceReferences.note: Used for standard and richer error models.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Retry
sourceReferences.note: Used for retry metrics and reliability controls.

sourceReferences.label: gRPC Docs: Health Checking
sourceReferences.note: Used for readiness and health-check standards.
